messages.content: # **Section 302 IPC: Definition, Punishment, and Essentials** **Applicable law:** Indian Penal Code, 1860 — Section 302 ## Direct Answer **[ESTABLISHED]** Section 302 of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) defines the offence of murder and prescribes punishment for the same. Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine [Indian Kanoon:1, Indian Kanoon:6]. The provision does not itself define "murder" but hinges on the definition provided under Section 300 IPC, which outlines the circumstances constituting murder [Indian Kanoon:1]. ## Key Points - Section 302 IPC provides punishment for murder, with sentencing alternatives of death or life imprisonment plus a fine [Indian Kanoon:1, Indian Kanoon:6]. - The offence hinges on the definition of "murder" under Section 300 IPC, which includes acts done with intent to cause death, knowledge of likely fatal consequences, or acts committed during dangerous bodily harm [Indian Kanoon:1]. - Trials under Section 302 are often conducted by Sessions Courts under Section 299 of the CrPC, and convictions frequently involve Section 34 IPC for joint liability where multiple accused share common intention [eSCR:4]. - Recent judgments reflect routine imposition of life imprisonment for murder convictions under Section 302 read with Section 34 IPC, with few cases receiving the death penalty [eSCR:4]. ## Analysis The statutory text of Section 302 IPC is unambiguous: it mandates capital punishment or life imprisonment for murder, but leaves sentencing discretion to courts based on aggravating and mitigating factors [Indian Kanoon:1, Indian Kanoon:6]. Courts frequently apply this section alongside Section 34 IPC when multiple accused are involved in a single murder, as seen in multiple High Court and Supreme Court judgments referenced in the sources [eSCR:4]. While Section 302 does not define murder, its meaning is derived from Section 300 IPC, which distinguishes murder from culpable homicide not amounting to murder [Indian Kanoon:1]. The absence of recent legislative amendments to Section 302 itself suggests that its application remains governed by judicial interpretation of Section 300 IPC and sentencing precedents [Indian Kanoon:1]. ## Practical Note Parties involved in murder trials should prepare for rigorous evidence assessment under Section 300 IPC to determine whether the offence falls under Section 302. Sentencing strategies should account for judicial preference toward life imprisonment unless aggravated circumstances warrant the death penalty, as reflected in recent judgments [eSCR:4]. ## Suggested Follow-up Queries 1. What are the key differences between Section 302 and Section 304 IPC in terms of punishment and essential ingredients? 2. How do courts determine whether to impose death sentence or life imprisonment under Section 302 IPC? 3. What role does Section 34 IPC play in joint liability for offences punishable under Section 302? 4. Are there any recent Supreme Court judgments (2023–2024) interpreting Section 302 IPC that alter its application? *This is AI-assisted legal research; consult a lawyer for advice.* ## Table of Authorities **Statutes** [India Code:2] [Access Denied](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/handle/123456789/15289?sam_handle=123456789/2489) ✅ fetched [India Code:3] [1501764525-THE-INDIAN-PENAL-CODE-1860](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/4219/1/THE-INDIAN-PENAL-CODE-1860.pdf) ⚠️ snippet only [India Code:5] [THE CODE OF CRIMINAL PROCEDURE, 1973 ACT NO. 2 OF 1974 [25th January, 1974.]](https://www.indiacode.nic.in/bitstream/123456789/4221/1/Criminal-Procedure-Code-CrPC-1973.pdf) ⚠️ snippet only **Case Law** [Indian Kanoon:1] [Indian Kanoon - Search engine for Indian Law](https://indiankanoon.org/search/?formInput=section+302+ipc) ✅ fetched [eSCR:4] [https://digiscr.sci.gov.in/html_view?dir=YWRtaW4va...](https://digiscr.sci.gov.in/html_view?dir=YWRtaW4vanVkZ2VtZW50X2ZpbGUvZWJvb2tzLzIwMjQvdm9sdW1lIDEvUGFydCBJLzIwMjQxMjQ4MjY2MTcwNTQ4OTUzOS5odG1s&judgment_id=MzY5OTc=) ⚠️ snippet only [Indian Kanoon:6] [Indian Kanoon - Search engine for Indian Law](https://indiankanoon.org/doc/1560742/) ✅ fetched

messages.content:
I can research **Section 302 IPC** from these focused angles. Please select the one that best fits your needs:

1. Punishment and sentencing trends under Section 302 IPC: Death penalty vs life imprisonment analysis with recent Supreme Court and High Court judgments (2023–2026)
2. Essential ingredients of murder under Section 302 IPC: How courts interpret Section 300 IPC in conjunction with Section 302, including landmark judgments
3. Role of Section 34 IPC in joint liability for offences punishable under Section 302: Recent judicial trends and case laws
4. Comparative analysis: Section 302 IPC vs Section 304 IPC (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) — punishment, ingredients, and judicial interpretation

Essential ingredients of murder under Section 302 IPC: How courts interpret Section 300 IPC in conjunction with Section 302, including landmark judgments
Role of Section 34 IPC in joint liability for offences punishable under Section 302: Recent judicial trends and case laws
Comparative analysis: Section 302 IPC vs Section 304 IPC (culpable homicide not amounting to murder) — punishment, ingredients, and judicial interpretation
